title: Cyberattack Wave
description: Banks and hospitals are hit by coordinated cyberattacks.

turns.speaker: security
turns.text: Emergency powers are necessary. Cyberattacks escalate quickly and hesitation creates national vulnerabilities.

turns.speaker: rights
turns.text: Emergency powers risk permanent surveillance creep. Freedom shouldn’t be collateral damage.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: Both of you are missing the economic angle. A shutdown of banks and hospitals costs billions.

turns.speaker: security
turns.text: Which is why decisive action matters. Cyberattacks don’t wait for committees.

turns.speaker: rights
turns.text: Private-sector partnerships protect rights while boosting defense.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: Partnerships help, but without government authority, coordination collapses.

title: Cyberattack Wave
description: Banks and hospitals are hit by coordinated cyberattacks.

turns.speaker: security
turns.text: The attacks are active — I urge 'Grant emergency cybersecurity powers' so responders can block damage and restore critical services now.

turns.speaker: rights
turns.text: Emergency powers risk overreach and data intrusion; we should instead fund long-term defenses with strict oversight to preserve civil liberties.

turns.speaker: security
turns.text: Brief, scoped emergency authority with sunset review stops immediate harm while we build resilience — it's not a blank check.

turns.speaker: rights
turns.text: Only if it's narrowly defined, transparent, and audited. Otherwise temporary powers become permanent surveillance tools.

turns.speaker: security
turns.text: So: limited emergency measures now, paired with a commitment to funding long-term defenses and independent audits.

turns.speaker: rights
turns.text: With those safeguards, I'm willing to accept a short, targeted response while we improve durable cybersecurity.
